action: Triage/TDD must bound per-cycle coverage-floor commitments by observed builder throughput. Evidence: three consecutive coverage cycles failed on the same shape — cycle 280 (3 tasks, builder starved cmd/evolve entirely, 'turn budget'), cycle 282 (3 tasks, Tasks 2+3 unimplemented, build PARTIAL), cycle 283 (3 tasks x ~12 package floors >=98%, build FAIL 'coverage floors not reached for all targets'). Observed sustainable throughput is ~4-5 package floors per builder turn (cycle 281 PASSed with exactly that scope). Fix direction: (a) triage caps committed coverage floors per cycle at the observed rolling throughput, splitting the remainder into deferred carryover; (b) TDD writes binding floor predicates ONLY for the floors triage committed this cycle (cycle-280 lesson: predicates gated deferred tasks); (c) optionally stage floors (88->92->95->98) across cycles instead of jumping to 98 everywhere at once.
evidence: .evolve/runs/cycle-280/retrospective-report.md (builder starved committed task while clearing deferred-task gates)
.evolve/runs/cycle-283/build-report.md (Status: FAIL - coverage floors not reached for all targets)
cycle 281 ship 02b778ef (PASS at ~5 floors: the throughput baseline)
